channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Son of Chinese journalist jailed for espionage calls for his father's release
item.description: The son of a Chinese journalist accused of espionage is calling for his father to be released from his seven-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Fu Ting, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Palestinians struggle to restart their lives in the ruins of Gaza
item.description: BEIT LAHIYA, Gaza Strip (AP) — When night falls over northern Gaza, much of the cityscape of collapsed buildings and piled wreckage turns pitch black.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED and ABDEL KAREEM HANA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands gather outside the Vatican to pray for Pope Francis' health
item.description: Thousands of people have gathered in St. Peter’s Square to pray for an ailing Pope Francis, expressing sorrow for his suffering, hope for his recovery and gratitude for his efforts to steer the Catholic Church in new directions.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell' Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Coca-Cola’s appeal to Palestinians fizzles as the Mideast war boosts demand for a local look-alike
item.description: The caramel brown liquid, red-and-white soda cans and splashy big font bear remarkable resemblance to an iconic American soft drink.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Most voters want Ukraine to reach settlement with Russia
item.description: Most voters want Ukraine to reach a settlement with Russia as the war between the two countries reaches its three-year anniversary, including a majority of Republicans, Democrats and independents, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Macron project unity despite clear Ukraine-Russia divide
item.description: President Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron sought to project unity during a White House meeting Monday, even as cracks opened further in the alliance between the U.S. and Europe over the path forward in Ukraine. The two met at the Oval Office as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Australian treasurer seeking tariff exemption in talks with Bessent
item.description: Australia’s treasurer, Jim Chalmers, will continue to push for steel and aluminum tariff exemptions in his discussions with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent this week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: Ukraine marks third anniversary of war as US shifts its policy under Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A dozen leaders from Europe and Canada are in Ukraine to mark the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion and discuss support with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. They include European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These 17 countries voted with US against Ukraine UN resolution
item.description: Seventeen countries in the United Nations General Assembly joined the U.S. on Monday in voting against a nonbinding resolution condemning Russia as the aggressor in the ongoing war in Ukraine — more than double the vote against a similar resolution two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Xi says China, Russia true friends as Trump pursues Putin talks
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping says China and Russia are true friends after speaking on the phone with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Pope shows slight improvement, resumes some work: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is showing a “slight improvement” in his condition and has resumed some of his work activities, according to the Vatican’s Monday evening bulletin. The pope remains in critical condition, as he undergoes treatment for pneumonia in both lungs.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: A police union in Haiti demands more protection a day after gangs kill a Kenyan policeman
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Haitian police officers on Monday demanded better protection and treatment a day after gunmen killed a Kenyan policeman who was part of a U.N.-backed mission tasked with fighting gangs in the troubled Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US votes against UN resolution condemning Russia for Ukraine war
item.description: The U.S. voted against a resolution condemning Russia as the aggressor in the war in Ukraine that passed the United Nations General Assembly on Monday, marking three years since Russia’s launched its full-scale invasion of the country. Among the 17 countries that joined the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: More pressure on Rwanda as Congo says rebel uprising has killed over 7,000 people this year
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — More than 7,000 people have died this year as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have captured unprecedented amounts of territory in mineral-rich eastern Congo, Congo's prime minister said Monday, as the European Union announced it would review an agreement with Rwanda on critical raw m
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and WILSON MCMAKIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Ukraine policy looks like an episode of 'The Sopranos': Democratic rep
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jim Himes (Conn.) says President Trump's push for Ukraine's rare earths and minerals amid the nation's war with Russia feels like an extortion plotline out of "The Sopranos.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: A witchcraft case involving Zambia's president brings scrutiny of a colonial-era law and traditions
item.description: LUSAKA, Zambia (AP) — Two men are on trial in Zambia on charges of practicing witchcraft and possessing charms intended to harm the country's president, in a case that has become a source of fascination in the southern African nation.
item.dc:creator: JACOB ZIMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian consulate in France targeted with explosive projectiles on 3rd anniversary of Ukraine invasion
item.description: Two explosive projectiles detonated outside the Russian consulate in Marseille, France, on the third anniversary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky praises ‘absolute heroism of Ukrainians’ three years after Russian invasion
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is praising the “absolute heroism” of his people on the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion. “Three years of resistance. Three years of gratitude. Three years of absolute heroism of Ukrainians. I am proud of Ukraine!” Zelensky posted to X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: On third anniversary of Ukraine invasion, European leaders show support, express unease
item.description: European leaders voiced support for Ukraine on the third anniversary of the Russia invasion while demanding a say in any US-Russia peace arrangement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' in kidney failure battle
item.description: Pope Francis' 10th night at Gemelli Hospital in Rome was quiet and restful as he battles early signs of kidney failure, the Vatican said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: 46.1 million people were counted in Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years
item.description: The final results of Iraq’s first census in nearly 40 years show the population has reached 46.1 million.
item.dc:creator: Qassim Abdul-Zahra, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis remains in critical condition but has shown slight improvement in laboratory tests.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former UN ambassador warns of potential repercussions of Russia-Ukraine rhetoric on China, Taiwan
item.description: Former United Nations ambassador Susan Rice warned that President Trump’s actions with ending the Russia-Ukraine war could have potential repercussions with China and Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Kenyan police officer sent to Haiti to help rein in violent gangs is killed in fighting
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A Kenyan police officer sent to Haiti to help rein in violent gangs was fatally shot in a fight with one of the gangs, Kenyan authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: PUERTO CARTÍ, Panama (AP) — They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s military breaks paramilitary group's siege of crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than yearlong siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffering from 'mild renal insufficiency,' though condition remains 'under control,' Vatican says
item.description: Vatican officials announced on Sunday that Pope Francis is currently suffering from "mild renal insufficiency" amid his treatment for bilateral pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
